interactions.final_answer:
## PHASE 0 — DESIGN PROPOSAL (IKEA intranet hub) I’ll build an IKEA “inside” hub that reflects how IKEA actually communicates: warm and practical, using **co-workers** language, highlighting **Democratic Design**, and publishing dense operational updates across the main audience channels—while keeping **policy/compliance** items gated with **read confirmation**. ### 1) SCOPE CONFIG - **Scope name:** **IKEA Inside** - **Scope description:**

IKEA Inside brings together the information co-workers need to do their best work—faster decisions, clearer policies, and practical updates across the IKEA concept.

- **Welcome message:**

Welcome to IKEA Inside—your place for operational updates, policy acknowledgements, and co-worker stories that make the many people mission real every day.

- **Scope hero brand logo (to upload in Phase 1.5):** `https://img.logo.dev/ikea.com?token=pk_OwST1E04QH-w3oM0YksM2Q&retina=true` **Brand colors (from profile mapping):** - primary_color: **#0058AB** - primary_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF** - secondary_color: **#FBD914** - warn_color: **#E00751** - warn_color_contrast: **#FFFFFF** - highlights: - **number_of_highlights:** 4 - **highlights_carousel:** true - **highlights_style:** BannerCarousel **Footer: 3 columns with real links from profile** - Column 1: **About** - https://about.ikea.com - https://www.ikeafoundation.org - Column 2: **Investor Relations** - https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ - Column 3: **Support** - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ **Social links (global official):** - LinkedIn: https://www.linkedin.com/company/ikea/ - Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/ikea/ - YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/ikea - X/Twitter: https://twitter.com/IKEA **navigation_links** - topBarLinks (3): - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ - https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ (Ingka newsroom) - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ (Inter IKEA newsroom) - footerLinks (5): - https://about.ikea.com - https://www.ikeafoundation.org - https://www.ingka.com/annual-summary-and-sustainability-report/ - https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/performance/ - https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - socialLinks: LinkedIn/Instagram/YouTube/X **components (choose 2 based on profile):** - **birthdays** (Tack!/people moments feel important culturally) - **upcomingEvents** (town halls/briefings/event programs) --- ### 2) CHANNEL ARCHITECTURE (5 channels) Primary channels are the 3 with most content + all pinned (is_sticky=true). Secondary channels hide highlights where appropriate. #### Ch1 (PRIMARY): “IKEA Inside: Daily Operational Updates” - **Purpose:** Timely, factual updates co-workers need—performance context, launches, and what’s changing on the ground. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#0058AB** - **is_sticky:** true - **image_url (stock match):** `stock/Message.png` - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** false - **hide_highlights:** false - **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** Reader - **FAQ (2-3):** 1) **Q: Where do I find the latest store-format updates?** **A:** Check the “City-format openings & changes” posts under this channel’s pinned items and weekly updates. If a change requires acknowledgement, it will be published in “Co-worker Policies & Security.” 2) **Q: Why are some updates written in a plain, direct style?** **A:** IKEA communications are designed to be understood and used quickly—short sentences, clear decisions, and what it means for co-workers and the many people we serve. #### Ch2 (PRIMARY): “Democratic Design & Product News” - **Purpose:** Product pipeline, innovation, and the practical meaning of Democratic Design—Form, Function, Quality, Sustainability, Low Price. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#0071BC** (digital accent) - **is_sticky:** true - **image_url (stock match):** `stock/Decision.png` - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** false - **hide_highlights:** false - **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** Reader - **FAQ (2-3):** 1) **Q: What does “cost-first” actually mean for co-workers?** **A:** Designers set the price target first, then engineer backwards—so affordability stays non-negotiable across the range. 2) **Q: Where do I read about sustainability targets and progress?** **A:** Look for sustainability progress updates in this channel, and policy acknowledgements in the compliance channel. #### Ch3 (PRIMARY): “Co-worker People & Culture” - **Purpose:** HR rhythms, learning, recognitions, and culture moments—including Togetherness and Tack! culture. - **Type:** public - **Color:** **#FBD914** (warm tone accent) - **is_sticky:** true - **image_url (stock match):** `stock/community.png` - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** false - **hide_highlights:** false - **Tabs:** ['Articles'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** Reader - **FAQ (2-3):** 1) **Q: I’m new—what should I read first for onboarding?** **A:** Start with the “Onboarding & learning: what to expect” guide and the latest learning notes pinned in this channel. 2) **Q: How are co-workers recognized?** **A:** Recognition runs locally (e.g., Co-worker of the Month) plus global programs like IKEA Talents. #### Ch4 (SECONDARY): “Co-worker Policies, Legal & Security” - **Purpose:** Compliance, security, and policy acknowledgements with clear “please read” requirements. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** **#E00751** - **is_sticky:** false - **image_url (stock match):** `stock/security.png` - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** true - **hide_highlights:** true - **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** None - **FAQ (2-3):** 1) **Q: What requires acknowledgement?** **A:** Legal/compliance/security policies and security best practices will require read confirmation before you can proceed. 2) **Q: Where do I find security guidance?** **A:** Under “Co-worker Policies, Legal & Security” with each item clearly marked as requiring acknowledgement when relevant. 3) **Q: How will I know which policy version I’ve read?** **A:** Each policy post includes the effective period/summary and triggers acknowledgement for that item. #### Ch5 (SECONDARY): “IT & Digital Working Practices” - **Purpose:** Practical digital guidance for co-workers—accounts, password practices, and working with digital tools. - **Type:** corporate - **Color:** **#0058AB** - **is_sticky:** false - **image_url (stock match):** `stock/Password.png` - **hide_on_homepage_feed:** true - **hide_highlights:** true - **Tabs:** ['Articles', 'Pages'] - **initial_tab:** 'Articles' - **default_role:** None - **FAQ (2-3):** 1) **Q: What should I do if I suspect a security issue?** **A:** Follow the “Report a concern safely” instructions and follow the acknowledgement steps on the security item. 2) **Q: Where do I learn the right way to work with digital content?** **A:** Guides in this channel cover safe sharing, permissions, and content review rhythms. --- ### 3) CONTENT CALENDAR (Published only in Phase 2) Stock-image assignment for content (from provided list) follows the channel topic. **Highlight rule:** Exactly **3–4 total** items across the whole hub must have `is_main_highlight=True`. I’m proposing **4 highlights total**: 1) A major price reduction / performance recap (Ch1) 2) Democratic Design + product pipeline update (Ch2) 3) Renewable electricity milestone (Ch1) 4) Restructuring context update (Ch3) #### Ch1 (PRIMARY) — 3 items **Ch1 Item A (Highlight, sticky):** - Title: **Prices down, market share up: FY24–FY25 recap in plain words** - Type: recap - tags: ['price-reductions', 'FY25', 'market-update', 'many-people'] - is_main_highlight: true - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: `stock/financial_report.png` - Body outline (one sentence): A direct, co-worker-focused recap explaining the €2.1B+ price reductions, FY24 revenue decline of 5.5%, FY25 continuing price lowering with Ingka revenue €41.5B, net profit €1.4B (+75%), and what it means for day-to-day work and service. **Ch1 Item B (sticky):** - Title: **Renewable electricity milestone: 94.8% matched in FY25 (and what “next” looks like)** - Type: milestone - tags: ['renewables', 'sustainability', 'FY25', 'energy'] - is_main_highlight: true - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/sustainability.png` - Body: 6–8 structured HTML sections explaining the FY25 94.8% figure toward 100%, the ~€4.2B renewable investments, and planning through 2030 (€3.3B planned), plus implications for stores and logistics. **Ch1 Item C:** - Title: **IKEA–Best Buy shop-in-shop pilot: lessons from the US expansion rhythm** - Type: spotlight - tags: ['US', 'partnership', 'store-format', 'accessibility'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/Idea.png` - Body: What’s changing for co-workers, why it matters for accessibility beyond large-format locations, and what “iteration now” looks like for the pilot. --- #### Ch2 (PRIMARY) — 3 items **Ch2 Item A (Highlight, sticky):** - Title: **Democratic Design in practice: we set the price target first—then engineer backwards** - Type: guide - tags: ['democratic-design', 'quality', 'low-price', 'design'] - is_main_highlight: true - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/Innovation.png` - Body: A practical guide explaining the 5 dimensions (Form/Function/Quality/Sustainability/Low Price), how cost-first design drives decisions, and examples tied to store-format learning. **Ch2 Item B:** - Title: **Oxford Street (May 2025) as a design case: city format, electric heating, and “As-Is”** - Type: spotlight - tags: ['city-format', 'Oxford Street', 'electric-heating', 'store-design'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/office.png` - Body: Uses the Oxford Street specifics (82,000 sq ft, six floors, air-source heat pumps, no car park, Swedish deli/restaurant, As-Is secondhand) and connects it back to Democratic Design choices. **Ch2 Item C:** - Title: **Circularity update: Sweden’s IKEA second-hand marketplace and how we expand it** - Type: milestone - tags: ['circularity', 'second-hand', 'Sweden', 'expansion'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: `stock/DigitalContent.png` - Body: Explains the January 2026 Sweden launch, expansion plan to Spain/Norway/Portugal/Poland, and how this complements in-store As-Is sections. --- #### Ch3 (PRIMARY) — 3 items **Ch3 Item A (Highlight, sticky):** - Title: **The leadership transition: Juvencio Maeztu and Jakub Jankowski—continuity with commercial urgency** - Type: announcement - tags: ['leadership', 'CEO', '2025-2026', 'direction'] - is_main_highlight: true - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: `stock/leadership_speech.png` - Body: Notes the dates (Maeztu since Nov 2025; Jankowski since Jan 2026), background (Madrid store manager; IKEA India; 20+ years; cross-country experience), and frames what co-workers can expect: simpler decision paths. **Ch3 Item B (sticky):** - Title: **Restructuring context: 800 Ingka office roles and 850 Inter IKEA roles—what “faster decisions” means** - Type: recap - tags: ['restructuring', 'Group Functions', 'Ingka', 'Inter IKEA'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: true - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: `stock/document_review.png` - Body: Explains March 2026 Ingka ~800 cuts (625 Sweden) and May 2026 Inter IKEA 850 cuts (~3% of 27,500; ~300 Sweden/Älmhult), clarifying not store co-workers, and linking to price reductions + tariff costs + consumer confidence. **Ch3 Item C:** - Title: **Tack! and Togetherness: how co-workers recognize one another across stores and hubs** - Type: spotlight - tags: ['Tack', 'Togetherness', 'recognition', 'culture'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/team_diversity.png` - Body: Explains Tack! pension contribution logic, Co-worker-of-the-Month, IKEA Talents, and how the learning platform supports ongoing growth. --- #### Ch4 (SECONDARY corporate/policy) — 2 items **Ch4 Item A (policy + acknowledgement):** - Title: **Information security basics: passwords, account hygiene, and reporting concerns** - Type: policy - tags: ['security', 'passwords', 'reporting', 'policy'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: true - status: Published - notify: true - image_url: `stock/Password.png` - Body: Covers practical do/don’t rules and incident reporting steps. Ends with CTA to confirm you’ve read. **Ch4 Item B (policy + acknowledgement):** - Title: **Responsible access & document review: getting approvals the IKEA way** - Type: policy - tags: ['document-review', 'approvals', 'governance', 'compliance'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: true - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/audit.png` - Body: Explains why approvals exist, how to keep it simple (bias for doing now), and how co-workers should approach document review and versioning. --- #### Ch5 (SECONDARY corporate/IT) — 2 items **Ch5 Item A (guide + acknowledgement):** - Title: **Working with digital content safely: review, sharing, and version discipline** - Type: guide - tags: ['digital-working', 'content', 'permissions', 'quality-check'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: true - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/QualityCheck.png` - Body: A practical checklist (review, correct audience, keep versions straight) tied to how IKEA avoids complexity. **Ch5 Item B (guide):** - Title: **Onboarding & learning rhythm: from classroom to digital platform (what to expect)** - Type: guide - tags: ['onboarding', 'learning', 'hybrid', 'welcome'] - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - status: Published - notify: false - image_url: `stock/onboarding.png` - Body: Explains combined on-the-job + classroom onboarding and continuing digital learning. --- ### 4) EVENTS (1–2 total across hub) I’m proposing **1 event** (keeps it realistic and not over-scheduled): **Event in Ch1 (PRIMARY, scheduled)** - Title: **Town hall: “How we side with the many people” — what’s changing and Q&A** - Type: event - tags: ['town-hall', 'qna', 'many-people', 'strategy'] - status: Published - notify: true - is_main_highlight: false - is_sticky: false - require_read_confirmation: false - image_url: `stock/Thinking.png` - event_start: **2026-09-15T14:00:00Z** - event_end: **2026-09-15T15:00:00Z** - location: **Älmhult (HQ), plus global live streams** - Body: Structured agenda + “bring your real questions” tone. *(If you want the event on a different date/time or location, tell me before we build.)* --- ## 5) HUB-LEVEL POLISH (global settings) - **company_name:** IKEA - **website_name:** IKEA - **default_language:** en-US (profile uses English primarily; HQ listed across Sweden/Netherlands; without explicit language in the profile, this is the safest default) - **supported_languages:** (not specified explicitly as a list in the profile; to avoid inventing, we can start with **['en-US']** unless you provide the exact set) - **login_quick_links (5 real URLs from profile):** 1) https://www.ikea.com/global/en/this-is-ikea/work-with-us/ 2) https://www.ingka.com/newsroom/ 3) https://www.inter.ikea.com/en/newsroom/ 4) https://about.ikea.com 5) https://www.ikea.com/global/en/our-business/sustainability/ - **sidebar_navigation (ordered):** ["collaborators", "documents", "workgroups", "learning", "approvalRequests"] (People-first + policy/document needs are prominent in this design.) --- # Confirmation request (STOP—no tools yet) This is a complete design. Shall I build it end-to-end, or would you like to adjust anything first — **channel names**, **content plan**, **colors**, **event date/time/location**, or **supported languages / quick links**?
